earlyVoting.notes: All registered voters receive a mail ballot automatically. Vote centers open starting 29 days before the election.

votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: California allows voters to use phones and electronic devices at polling places, including to reference voting information. Photography of other voters is prohibited.
additionalRules: All registered voters receive a mail-in ballot automatically
Top-two primary system: all candidates appear on one ballot regardless of party; top two advance to the general election

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: California uses a top-two nonpartisan primary (Proposition 14, 2010). All registered voters may vote for any candidate regardless of party. The top two vote-getters advance to the general election.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: California utiliza una primaria no partidista de los dos primeros (Proposición 14, 2010). Todos los votantes registrados pueden votar por cualquier candidato sin importar el partido. Los dos candidatos con más votos avanzan a las elecciones generales.
